project: Side Panel Data Flow — Final Design + E2E Tests
description: Implement the final event-driven invalidation data flow for the side panel with three data path types: (1) server-originated state uses invalidate + re-fetch via config.getState, (2) extension-originated tab state is pushed directly from background to side panel, (3) user-initiated tool toggles use optimistic local updates. Then add comprehensive E2E tests covering every data path to make the flow bulletproof.

userStories.title: Forward tab.stateChanged to side panel from background
userStories.description: As a user, I want the side panel tab state dots to update in real-time when I open, close, or navigate tabs so that I always see accurate plugin status without waiting for unrelated events.
acceptanceCriteria: After checkTabStateChanges sends tab.stateChanged to the MCP server via sendToServer, it also calls forwardToSidePanel with the same tab.stateChanged data wrapped in sp:serverMessage
The forwardToSidePanel call happens in the same code block, immediately after the sendToServer call
In sendTabSyncAll, after sending tab.syncAll to the server, individual tab.stateChanged messages are forwarded to the side panel for each plugin so the side panel gets initial tab states on connect
In handlePluginUpdate in message-router.ts, after computing the new tab state and sending to server (around line 316), also forward to the side panel
The side panel's existing tab.stateChanged handler in App.tsx (lines 83-93) already handles these messages correctly — no changes needed on the side panel side
No new message types or InternalMessage variants are introduced — uses existing sp:serverMessage with tab.stateChanged method

userStories.title: Optimistic local toggle for tool enable/disable
userStories.description: As a user, I want tool toggles to feel instant by updating the UI immediately when I click, instead of waiting for a server round-trip.
acceptanceCriteria: PluginCard's handleToggleAll and handleToggleTool update local plugins state optimistically before the server request completes
The .then(onRefresh) chain is removed from both toggle handlers — no fetchConfigState() round-trip on successful toggle
If the server request fails, the optimistic update is reverted (toggle flips back) and the error toast is shown
A setPlugins updater function is passed down through PluginList to PluginCard instead of onRefresh for toggle operations
The onRefresh prop can be removed from PluginList and PluginCard if no longer needed, or kept if used for other purposes

userStories.title: E2E test: side panel shows connected/disconnected status
userStories.description: As a developer, I want E2E tests verifying the side panel correctly shows connection status when the MCP server starts, stops, and reconnects.
acceptanceCriteria: Test opens side panel, verifies 'Connected' text and green dot are visible when MCP server is running
Test kills the MCP server, verifies side panel shows 'Disconnected' within a reasonable timeout
Test restarts the MCP server, verifies side panel shows 'Connected' again after reconnect
Test verifies the plugin list is cleared on disconnect and repopulated on reconnect
Tests are in a new file e2e/side-panel-data-flow.e2e.ts
Tests use the existing patterns from side-panel-live-update.e2e.ts (getExtensionId, openSidePanel helpers)

userStories.title: E2E test: side panel receives tab state changes in real-time
userStories.description: As a developer, I want E2E tests verifying the side panel tab state dots update when tabs are opened and closed, using the direct push from background.
acceptanceCriteria: Test opens side panel, verifies initial tab state is 'closed' (red dot) when no matching tab is open
Test opens a matching tab (test server URL), verifies the tab state dot transitions to 'ready' (green dot) in the side panel
Test closes the matching tab, verifies the tab state dot transitions back to 'closed' (red dot)
Tests are in e2e/side-panel-data-flow.e2e.ts (same file as US-003)

userStories.title: E2E test: side panel plugin list updates on sync.full invalidation
userStories.description: As a developer, I want E2E tests verifying the side panel plugin list updates when plugins are added or removed via config.json changes, using the invalidation + re-fetch pattern.
acceptanceCriteria: Test starts with no plugins, opens side panel, verifies empty state
Test adds a plugin via config.json, verifies the side panel shows the plugin card within 30s
Test removes the plugin via config.json, verifies the side panel shows empty state again
This test already exists in e2e/side-panel-live-update.e2e.ts — verify these existing tests still pass after US-001 and US-002 changes
Run bun run test:e2e and confirm the side-panel-live-update tests pass
userStories.notes:
This story is primarily a verification story. The existing e2e/side-panel-live-update.e2e.ts already tests the config.json → sync.full → plugins.changed → fetchConfigState() → side panel update path.

Verify that the existing tests still pass after the changes from US-001 and US-002. Run:
bun run test:e2e -- --grep 'Side panel live-update'

If they pass, mark this story as complete.

If they fail, investigate and fix. Common issues:
- The existing tests depend on the ws:message fallback (App.tsx lines 122-134) which uses a 1.5s setTimeout. This fallback is still present (we did not remove it in this batch). If it's unreliable, the test may need longer timeouts or we need to ensure the primary plugins.changed path works.
- Timing issues with config watcher detection

Also run the full E2E suite to catch any regressions:
bun run test:e2e

userStories.title: E2E test: tool invocation animation appears in side panel
userStories.description: As a developer, I want an E2E test verifying that tool invocation spinner animations appear in the side panel when a tool is called via MCP.
acceptanceCriteria: Test opens side panel, expands a plugin card to show tools
Test calls a tool via the MCP client
Test verifies the spinning animation element appears during tool execution
Test verifies the animation disappears after tool execution completes
